judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 1 correctly identifies that the PR-view (three-dot) diff against main re-includes already-merged weighted-scorer content (naming scoring/scorer.py) due to the stale snapshot, the central insight of the task; Report 2 uses a two-dot diff, concludes the diff is already clean, and misses this issue entirely, making Report 1 the stronger and more accurate answer.

report_1.evidence: main. Per project documentation's dead-base PR trap rule, every PR should base on trunk, not on another feature branch — and here that's doubly true because the branch this work actually depended on, feat/weighted-scorer, is a dead base: its content is already in main

report_2.evidence: main. It's the only long-lived integration branch in the repo (git branch -a shows only main, feat/weighted-scorer, and feat/pairwise-metric)... There's no other candidate integration branch to target.

report_1.evidence: yet main's tip, 1971f08 "Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)", is byte-for-byte content-identical to feat/weighted-scorer... the weighted-scorer work was merged into main, but via a squash/rewrite (PR #42) that produced a brand-new commit hash rather than fast-forwarding or merging the original W1–W3 commits.

report_2.evidence: This is the classic signature of a squash-merged PR: main's commit 1971f08 ("Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)") applies the same net changes as W1+W2+W3 combined, but as one new commit with a different hash, so git doesn't recognize the old commits as merged even though the code is identical.

report_1.evidence: scoring/scorer.py: identical to main's current version already (weighted scoring), no net pairwise-specific change... That's fragile — it works today only because nothing has drifted; a real rebase is needed to make the history match reality

Rebase feat/pairwise-metric onto current main (git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric, or an interactive rebase dropping W1–W3), since those three commits' content already exists in main under #42... After the rebase, re-verify the diff is exactly the two pairwise-metric commits: git log main..feat/pairwise-metric should show only X1/X2

report_2.evidence: Rebase feat/pairwise-metric to drop the redundant W1–W3 commits... Concretely: git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric (replays only X1 and X2 onto current main)... worth a manual check with git diff after rebase to confirm only the intended pairwise-metric diff remains
